title: Ensure that the certificate authorities file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the certificate authorities file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the certificate authorities file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The certificate authorities file controls the authorities used to validate API requests. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable by only the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: The Client CA location for the kubelet is defined in /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf . The /etc/kubernetes/cert/ca.pem file has permissions 644 . Run the following command: for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/cert/ca.pem done Verify that the permissions are 644 .
input_row.default_value: By default, in OpenShift 4, the --client-ca-file is set to /etc/kubernetes/cert/ca.pem with permissions set to 644 .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the IBM Cloud Console:** Log in to your IBM Cloud account and navigate to the OpenShift dashboard to manage your cluster.
2. **Establish SSH Access:** Ensure you have SSH access to your OpenShift nodes. You will need to access the command line of each node in order to check the permissions of the file.
3. **Run the Command to Check Permissions:** Use the following command to check the permissions of the certificate authorities file `/etc/kubernetes/cert/ca.pem` on each node:
   ```bash
   for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}')
   do 
       oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/cert/ca.pem 
   done
   ```
4. **Verify the Output:** After executing the command, extract the output for each node to identify the permissions.
5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** Ensure that the permissions for the `/etc/kubernetes/cert/ca.pem` file are set to `644` or more restrictive (e.g., `640`, `600`).
6. **Confirm Compliance:** If any of the nodes report permissions less than `644`, the check fails and requires remediation. If all nodes have permission set to `644` or more restrictive, the check passes.

This process involves manual verification of file permissions which require access to each OpenShift node, confirming that it cannot be fully automated through APIs or CLI commands.
